package handler

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

// Positive scenario for api_examples_embedded_in_handlers_instead_of_transport_docs_helpers.
// Rule intent: Long example payload blocks should live in docs or example helpers,
// not hardcoded as comments inside handler functions.
// Anti-pattern: Request/response examples embedded directly in handler comments.

type UserHandler struct{}

// CreateUser handles POST /users
//
// Example: POST /users
// Request:
//
//	{
//	  "name": "John Doe",
//	  "email": "john@example.com",
//	  "role": "admin",
//	  "metadata": {
//	    "department": "engineering",
//	    "location": "NYC"
//	  }
//	}
//
// Response 201:
//
//	{
//	  "id": "usr_123",
//	  "name": "John Doe",
//	  "email": "john@example.com",
//	  "role": "admin",
//	  "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
//	}
//
// Response 400:
//
//	{
//	  "error": "validation_failed",
//	  "fields": ["email"]
//	}
func (h *UserHandler) CreateUser(c *gin.Context) {
	var req struct {
		Name  string `json:"name" binding:"required"`
		Email string `json:"email" binding:"required,email"`
		Role  string `json:"role" binding:"oneof=admin user moderator"`
	}
	if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&req); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "validation_failed"})
		return
	}
	c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, gin.H{"id": "usr_123", "name": req.Name})
}

// UpdateOrder handles PATCH /orders/:id
//
// Example: PATCH /orders/ord_456
// Request:
//
//	{
//	  "status": "shipped",
//	  "tracking_number": "1Z999AA10123456784",
//	  "carrier": "ups"
//	}
//
// Response 200:
//
//	{
//	  "id": "ord_456",
//	  "status": "shipped",
//	  "tracking_number": "1Z999AA10123456784"
//	}
func (h *UserHandler) UpdateOrder(c *gin.Context) {
	var req struct {
		Status         string `json:"status" binding:"required"`
		TrackingNumber string `json:"tracking_number"`
	}
	if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&req); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "invalid payload"})
		return
	}
	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"id": c.Param("id"), "status": req.Status})
}
